title: Regarding the Application of Wet Signature to A.TR Circulation Documents
bodyHtml:
In the letter dated 2/5/2024 by the Directorate General of Customs, it was notified that until a second announcement,  customs visa field of A.TR Circulation Documents (box 12), which are electronically issued via MEDOS and contain QR codes, should also be signed by customs officials.

Regarding this issue, a new letter received from the European Commission states:

A.TR Circulation Documents issued before May 1, 2024, and which can be presented to European Union (EU) customs after May 1, 2024, due to reasons such as the goods being in transit, being in temporary storage, being in a bonded warehouse, etc., and do not contain wet signatures, will be considered as having wet signatures. For this purpose, a general acceptance text prepared by the Ministry of Trade will be submitted to the European Commission.

In this context, there is no obstacle to the acceptance of A.TR Circulation Documents without wet signatures by EU customs, provided that they were issued before May 1, 2024.

Click here for the related letter of Directorate General.

Click here for the European Commission's notice.
